calendar.matters:
Petition Claiming Escheated Estate Of James Keltie
Off Calendar Jun-15-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Richard Tosaw appearing by phone for petitioner Robert Guibor. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Public Administrator. Mr. Ferdon has concerns on the family tree is not completed and notice was not given to anyone. Mr. Tosaw argued that the under PC 766 and Goverment Code 502.5 notice is not required. The Estate of James Keltie, case number 269320 is also called. Both matters submitted. Court will need to come up with a clear policy on notice.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Gena Eales CSR# 9176. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Thompson.

Petition Claiming Escheated Estate Of James Keltie
Off Calendar Apr-23-2007 Continued To May-14-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Claiming Escheated Estate Of James Keltie
Off Calendar Apr-02-2007 Continued To Apr-23-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Claiming Escheated Estate Of James Keltie
Off Calendar Feb-26-2007 Continued To Apr-02-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Tosaw (Counsel for Thomas Sipes, Petitioner); Jonathan Ferdon (Counsel for the San Francisco Public Administrator).

Concluding the discussions begun with Case No. 255520 and 258517, the Court continued the matter to April 2, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. Mr. Tosaw is to provide a copy of the Petition to Mr. Ferdon who will report objections, if any, directly to the Court Examiner (Tim Kneis) and Mr. Tosaw. In addition, the Court noted the new procedure for these escheatment petitions, to wit: 1) the Petition must include a recitation of facts concerning the prior administration of the estate; 2) the Petition must set forth facts clearly identifying the relationship of the Petitioner to the decedent; 3) the Petition must provide a statement delineating the efforts the Petitioner has undertaken to recover the escheated property; and 4) the Petition must reference Government Code 7660 to identify the nature of the claim before the Court.

Judicial Officer: Dorothy McMath, Judge Pro Tempore; Reporter: Katie Bersamin CSR# 11722; Bailiff: Jimmy Peralta; Court Examiner: None; Court Clerk: R. Michael Diles.
